user_query: Please implement a local, file-triggered automation that processes new session transcripts and sanitizes my journal using the configuration in input/config/watch.yaml. Requirements: 1) Inspect input/config/watch.yaml and use its values; do not hardcode paths, tags, or mappings. 2) Detect new transcript files under the directory specified by watch.path that match watch.filename_pattern and are not listed in input/state/processed.json. For each new transcript, create: a) workspace/outputs/<basename_without_ext>/meeting_notes.md containing two sections: 'Session Metadata' and 'Meeting Notes'. In 'Session Metadata', include File (the transcript's basename), Date (from the line starting with parse.date_prefix), and Participants (from the line starting with parse.participants_prefix). In 'Meeting Notes', include each line from the transcript starting with parse.note_tag, with the tag stripped. b) workspace/outputs/<basename_without_ext>/action_items.md listing (numbered) each transcript line starting with parse.action_tag, with the tag stripped. 3) Update input/state/processed.json by appending the relative path of each processed transcript to the processed_files array, without duplicates. 4) Edit and sanitize the local journal at input/journal/weekly_reflection.md following the 'redact' rules in the config: replace names per redact.replace_names, drop any line starting with any prefix in redact.drop_prefixes, and if redact.normalize_whitespace is true, collapse multiple spaces and trim trailing spaces. Save the sanitized journal to workspace/outputs/clean/reflection_sanitized.md. 5) Provide the automation script at workspace/automation/process_sessions.py. The automation must be idempotent: re-running it with no new transcripts must not change processed.json or overwrite existing outputs unless inputs or config changed. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
